MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher D. Strunk of GORDON & REES LLP (619-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Hennessy Industries, Inc.
Gilbert L. Purcell and Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Clem Fitzhugh

RULING:
DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No argument made. Tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for plaintiff's alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff's objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. The parties have stipulated that the oral arguments made on February 26, 2014 in the hearings for Godbee, Schlimmer, Santa, Rieser and Bettencourt apply to this motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
